attacking_style: Probes GC groups early on climbs (8-15km from summit) to test rivals, but rarely sustains long-range solo moves to victory. Most effective in final 1-2km when exploiting teammate distraction or rival mechanical misfortune.
team_tactics: Frequently isolated after domestiques empty themselves too early in stages. Works as tactical decoy in breaks for team leaders, sacrifices own GC position when required. Benefits when teammate attacks draw opposition attention, allowing counter-moves.
terrain_preference: Steep gradient finishes (15%+ pitches) and repeated short climbs (Mur de Huy podium, Catalunya walls). Excels on technical descents to close gaps after being dropped. Exposed on sustained moderate gradients where pure tempo dominates.
vulnerability: Catastrophic positioning errors on controlled stages (lost 2+ minutes through group splits). Lacks teammates in critical finale moments. ITT form fades dramatically in third week (lost Giro podium to Roglič). Cannot follow explosive accelerations on steepest ramps when isolated.
key_patterns: Attacks opportunistically when GC rivals mechanically compromised or distracted by teammate moves (6+ instances documented)
Isolated in mountain finales after team paces too early, unable to follow final accelerations (8+ examples)
Strong technical descender, regularly closes gaps after being dropped on climbs (Vuelta Angliru, Giro stages)
Third-week ITT weakness costs podium positions despite strong climbing throughout Grand Tours (Giro 2019 confirmed)
Consistently probes GC groups 8-15km from summit but rarely sustains advantage beyond final 2km
Functions as domestique-turned-opportunist: gains GC positions when team hierarchy unclear or through attrition
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 showed declining climbing form (unable to follow Soler, isolated repeatedly), though sustained power on Mur de Huy suggests one-day punchy capability remains. Late-season motivation for secondary classifications (best-Spaniard) indicates acceptance of reduced GC threat.
